clusters.justification: United by co-activation in the limbic and salience circuitry (e.g., amygdala, anterior insula, ventral striatum), these terms reflect the overarching macroscopic domain of emotional valence, arousal, and mood processing.

Driving localized activity in the striatum, orbitofrontal cortex, and broader reward circuitry, this cluster represents the high-level functional axis of computing subjective value to guide choices and motivated behavior.

Bound by robust co-activation across the default mode network (e.g., medial prefrontal cortex, temporoparietal junction, posterior cingulate), this axis supports internally generated, referential thought directed at the self and others.
